data.description:
Sibling of the ready --json fix (23d2-e0f3 follow-up, commit c6a67e7), found auditing the read arms.

## Antipattern
A read arm accepts and silently ignores unknown/removed options instead of rejecting them, so a user passing a removed legacy flag (or a typo) gets silently wrong/ignored behavior. ready was fixed to reject; list and show already reject; search and deps still tolerate.

## Locations
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket_reads.py _cmd_search: explicit `elif arg.startswith("--"): continue  # tolerate unknown flags (matches the old shim)`.
- _cmd_deps: only reads positional + --include-archived; any other --flag is silently dropped.

## Experiment (confirmed)
On a real repo:
- `rebar search probe --json` -> rc=0, silently accepted (--json ignored; json is default so it "works by accident").
- `rebar deps <id> --json` -> rc=0, silently accepted.
- `rebar deps <id> --bogus-flag` -> rc=0, silently accepted.
Compare: `rebar list --json` / `list --bogus-flag` -> rc=1 "unknown option"; `ready --json` -> rc=2 (fixed).

## Fix
Reject unknown --options in _cmd_search and _cmd_deps (exit non-zero + usage), matching list/show/ready. Keep known flags (search: --status/--type/--has-tag/--include-archived; deps: --include-archived). Decide whether the search "old shim" tolerance is still desired (likely not, post-collapse).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] search/deps reject unknown options (RED test first)
- [ ] known flags still work; output unchanged
- [ ] guard consistent across all read arms
data.title: read arms search/deps silently ignore unknown options (e.g. removed legacy --json) — same as the ready --json fix
